headline: Models Split on SPY Direction While Tech Consensus Delivers Mixed Results
summary: A day of divided predictions saw models disagreeing sharply on SPY's direction, with Claude correctly calling it down while three others expected gains. Tech stock predictions showed better consensus on QQQ's upward move, though NVDA surprised all bullish models by declining despite strong AI infrastructure spending narratives.

consensus_picks.note: Claude stood alone predicting SPY's decline citing Fed volatility, while GPT-4o, Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity all incorrectly expected gains.
Three models correctly anticipated QQQ's rise based on tech sector momentum and positive premarket futures.
All three models wrongly predicted NVDA gains despite citing strong AI capex tailwinds, with the stock declining to $209.25.
Both models incorrectly predicted Apple gains, with the stock actually falling to $270.17.

best_call.summary: Claude's contrarian SPY short call based on Fed meeting volatility patterns proved prescient while four other models got it wrong.

worst_call.summary: Despite citing compelling $600B+ hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending, Claude's high-confidence NVDA bull call missed badly as the stock fell 4.7%.
